     1	// MODULE: Deep-Loop Executor Config
     2	
     3	import { z } from '../../../system-spec-kit/mcp_server/node_modules/zod/index.js';
     4	
     5	// ───── TYPE DEFINITIONS ─────
     6	
     7	export const EXECUTOR_KINDS = ['native', 'cli-codex', 'cli-gemini', 'cli-claude-code', 'cli-opencode', 'cli-devin'] as const;
     8	export type ExecutorKind = typeof EXECUTOR_KINDS[number];
     9	
    10	export const REASONING_EFFORTS = ['none', 'minimal', 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'xhigh'] as const;
    11	export type ReasoningEffort = typeof REASONING_EFFORTS[number];
    12	
    13	export const SERVICE_TIERS = ['priority', 'standard', 'fast'] as const;
    14	export type ServiceTier = typeof SERVICE_TIERS[number];
    15	
    16	const SANDBOX_MODES = ['read-only', 'workspace-write', 'danger-full-access'] as const;
    17	export type SandboxMode = typeof SANDBOX_MODES[number];
    18	export type GeminiSandboxMode = 'docker' | 'none';
    19	export type ClaudePermissionMode = 'plan' | 'acceptEdits' | 'bypassPermissions';
    20	
    21	// ───── CONSTANTS ─────
    22	
    23	export const executorConfigSchema = z.object({
    24	  kind: z.enum(EXECUTOR_KINDS).default('native'),
    25	  model: z.string().min(1).nullable().default(null),
    26	  reasoningEffort: z.enum(REASONING_EFFORTS).nullable().default(null),
    27	  serviceTier: z.enum(SERVICE_TIERS).nullable().default(null),
    28	  sandboxMode: z.enum(SANDBOX_MODES).nullable().default(null),
    29	  timeoutSeconds: z.number().int().positive().default(900),
    30	});
    31	
    32	export type ExecutorConfig = z.infer<typeof executorConfigSchema>;
    33	
    34	export const EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT: Record<ExecutorKind, readonly (keyof ExecutorConfig)[]> = {
    35	  native: [],
    36	  'cli-codex': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'serviceTier', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    37	  'cli-gemini': ['model', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    38	  'cli-claude-code': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    39	  'cli-opencode': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    40	  'cli-devin': ['model', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
    41	};
    42	
    43	export const GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS = ['gemini-3.1-pro-preview'] as const;
    44	export type GeminiSupportedModel = typeof GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS[number];
    45	
    46	export const DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS = ['swe-1.6', 'deepseek-v4', 'glm-5.1', 'kimi-k2.6'] as const;
    47	export type DevinSupportedModel = typeof DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS[number];
    48	export type DevinPermissionMode = 'auto' | 'dangerous';
    49	
    50	// ───── DOMAIN ERRORS ─────
    51	
    52	type ExecutorConfigIssue = {
    53	  path: PropertyKey[];
    54	  message: string;
    55	};
    56	
    57	export class ExecutorNotWiredError extends Error {
    58	  kind: Extract<ExecutorKind, 'cli-gemini' | 'cli-claude-code'>;
    59	
    60	  constructor(kind: Extract<ExecutorKind, 'cli-gemini' | 'cli-claude-code'>) {
    61	    super(`Executor kind '${kind}' is reserved in the schema but not yet wired. Awaiting future spec for ${kind} integration.`);
    62	    this.name = 'ExecutorNotWiredError';
    63	    this.kind = kind;
    64	  }
    65	}
    66	
    67	export class ExecutorConfigError extends Error {
    68	  issues: ExecutorConfigIssue[];
    69	
    70	  constructor(input: { issues: ExecutorConfigIssue[] }) {
    71	    super(input.issues.map((issue) => `${issue.path.map(String).join('.') || '<root>'}: ${issue.message}`).join('; '));
    72	    this.name = 'ExecutorConfigError';
    73	    this.issues = input.issues;
    74	  }
    75	}
    76	
    77	// ───── HELPERS ─────
    78	
    79	function isRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {
    80	  return value !== null && !Array.isArray(value) && typeof value === 'object';
    81	}
    82	
    83	function normalizeSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): SandboxMode {
    84	  return mode ?? 'workspace-write';
    85	}
    86	
    87	function normalizeExecutorConfigInput(raw: unknown): unknown {
    88	  if (!isRecord(raw) || !Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(raw, 'type')) {
    89	    return raw;
    90	  }
    91	
    92	  const legacyKind = raw.type;
    93	  if (typeof legacyKind !== 'string') {
    94	    return raw;
    95	  }
    96	
    97	  if (typeof raw.kind === 'string' && raw.kind !== legacyKind) {
    98	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
    99	      issues: [{
   100	        path: ['type'],
   101	        message: `deprecated executor field 'type' conflicts with canonical kind '${raw.kind}'`,
   102	      }],
   103	    });
   104	  }
   105	
   106	  console.warn("[executor-config] Deprecated executor field 'type' was provided; use 'kind' instead.");
   107	  const { type: _legacyType, ...rest } = raw;
   108	  return {
   109	    ...rest,
   110	    kind: typeof raw.kind === 'string' ? raw.kind : legacyKind,
   111	  };
   112	}
   113	
   114	function normalizeIssues(error: z.ZodError<ExecutorConfig>): ExecutorConfigIssue[] {
   115	  return error.issues.map((issue) => ({
   116	    path: issue.path,
   117	    message: issue.message,
   118	  }));
   119	}
   120	
   121	// ───── EXPORTS ─────
   122	
   123	/**
   124	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Codex CLI sandbox mode.
   125	 *
   126	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   127	 * @returns Codex-compatible sandbox mode.
   128	 */
   129	export function resolveCodexSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): SandboxMode {
   130	  return normalizeSandboxMode(mode);
   131	}
   132	
   133	/**
   134	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Gemini CLI sandbox mode.
   135	 *
   136	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   137	 * @returns 'docker' for workspace-write/read-only, 'none' for danger-full-access.
   138	 */
   139	export function resolveGeminiSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): GeminiSandboxMode {
   140	  return normalizeSandboxMode(mode) === 'danger-full-access' ? 'none' : 'docker';
   141	}
   142	
   143	/**
   144	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Claude Code permission mode.
   145	 *
   146	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   147	 * @returns Claude-compatible permission mode.
   148	 */
   149	export function resolveClaudePermissionMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): ClaudePermissionMode {
   150	  switch (normalizeSandboxMode(mode)) {
   151	    case 'read-only':
   152	      return 'plan';
   153	    case 'danger-full-access':
   154	      return 'bypassPermissions';
   155	    default:
   156	      return 'acceptEdits';
   157	  }
   158	}
   159	
   160	/**
   161	 * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Devin CLI permission mode.
   162	 *
   163	 * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
   164	 * @returns 'auto' for workspace-write/read-only, 'dangerous' for danger-full-access.
   165	 */
   166	export function resolveDevinPermissionMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): DevinPermissionMode {
   167	  return normalizeSandboxMode(mode) === 'danger-full-access' ? 'dangerous' : 'auto';
   168	}
   169	
   170	/**
   171	 * Parse and validate a raw executor configuration.
   172	 *
   173	 * Normalizes legacy `type` fields, validates against the Zod schema,
   174	 * and enforces executor-kind-specific field support.
   175	 *
   176	 * @param raw - Raw input to parse (JSON-parsed object).
   177	 * @returns Validated ExecutorConfig.
   178	 * @throws {@link ExecutorConfigError} If validation fails.
   179	 */
   180	export function parseExecutorConfig(raw: unknown): ExecutorConfig {
   181	  const parsed = executorConfigSchema.safeParse(normalizeExecutorConfigInput(raw));
   182	  if (!parsed.success) {
   183	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   184	      issues: normalizeIssues(parsed.error),
   185	    });
   186	  }
   187	
   188	  const config = parsed.data;
   189	  if (config.kind === 'cli-codex' && config.model === null) {
   190	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   191	      issues: [{ path: ['model'], message: 'model is required when kind is cli-codex' }],
   192	    });
   193	  }
   194	  if (config.kind === 'cli-devin' && config.model === null) {
   195	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   196	      issues: [{ path: ['model'], message: 'model is required when kind is cli-devin' }],
   197	    });
   198	  }
   199	
   200	  const supportedFlags = EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT[config.kind];
   201	  const unsupportedFields: string[] = [];
   202	  const allOptionalFields: (keyof ExecutorConfig)[] = [
   203	    'model',
   204	    'reasoningEffort',
   205	    'serviceTier',
   206	    'sandboxMode',
   207	    'timeoutSeconds',
   208	  ];
   209	
   210	  for (const field of allOptionalFields) {
   211	    if (!supportedFlags.includes(field) && config[field] !== null && !(field === 'timeoutSeconds' && config[field] === 900)) {
   212	      unsupportedFields.push(field);
   213	    }
   214	  }
   215	
   216	  if (unsupportedFields.length > 0) {
   217	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   218	      issues: unsupportedFields.map((field) => ({
   219	        path: [field],
   220	        message: `field '${field}' is not supported by executor kind '${config.kind}'. Supported fields for ${config.kind}: ${
   221	          supportedFlags.length ? supportedFlags.join(', ') : 'none'
   222	        }.`,
   223	      })),
   224	    });
   225	  }
   226	
   227	  if (
   228	    config.kind === 'cli-gemini' &&
   229	    config.model !== null &&
   230	    !GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS.some((model) => model === config.model)
   231	  ) {
   232	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   233	      issues: [
   234	        {
   235	          path: ['model'],
   236	          message: `model '${config.model}' is not a supported cli-gemini model. Supported: ${GEMINI_SUPPORTED_MODELS.join(', ')}.`,
   237	        },
   238	      ],
   239	    });
   240	  }
   241	
   242	  if (
   243	    config.kind === 'cli-devin' &&
   244	    config.model !== null &&
   245	    !DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS.some((model) => model === config.model)
   246	  ) {
   247	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   248	      issues: [
   249	        {
   250	          path: ['model'],
   251	          message: `model '${config.model}' is not a supported cli-devin model. Supported: ${DEVIN_SUPPORTED_MODELS.join(', ')}.`,
   252	        },
   253	      ],
   254	    });
   255	  }
   256	
   257	  return config;
   258	}
   259	
   260	/**
   261	 * Resolve an executor configuration by merging CLI and file sources.
   262	 *
   263	 * @param sources - CLI and file partial configs (CLI takes precedence).
   264	 * @returns Validated ExecutorConfig.
   265	 */
   266	export function resolveExecutorConfig(sources: {
   267	  cli?: Partial<ExecutorConfig>;
   268	  file?: Partial<ExecutorConfig>;
   269	}): ExecutorConfig {
   270	  const merged = {
   271	    ...(sources.file ?? {}),
   272	    ...(sources.cli ?? {}),
   273	  };
   274	  return parseExecutorConfig(merged);
   275	}
   276	
   277	// ───── FAN-OUT (MULTI-EXECUTOR) CONFIG ─────
   278	//
   279	// Opt-in layer ABOVE the single-executor path. When a fan-out config is present,
   280	// the loop runs N executor "lineages" concurrently (capped), each running the
   281	// existing sequential loop in its own isolated sub-packet. The single-executor
   282	// path (executorConfigSchema / parseExecutorConfig) is untouched and remains the
   283	// default; callers use EITHER a single `executor` OR a `fanout` block, never both.
   284	
   285	const LINEAGE_LABEL_PATTERN = /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/;
   286	
   287	/**
   288	 * One fan-out lineage: a single executor config plus fan-out metadata.
   289	 *
   290	 * - `label`: directory-safe id for the lineage's isolated sub-packet.
   291	 * - `count`: number of replicas of THIS config to run (e.g. 5x the same model).
   292	 * - `iterations`: per-lineage max-iterations override; null = packet default.
   293	 */
   294	export const lineageExecutorSchema = executorConfigSchema.extend({
   295	  label: z.string().min(1).regex(LINEAGE_LABEL_PATTERN, {
   296	    message: "label must match /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/ (lowercase, digits, hyphens; dir-safe)",
   297	  }),
   298	  count: z.number().int().positive().default(1),
   299	  iterations: z.number().int().positive().nullable().default(null),
   300	});
   301	
   302	export type LineageExecutor = z.infer<typeof lineageExecutorSchema>;
   303	
   304	export const fanoutConfigSchema = z.object({
   305	  executors: z.array(lineageExecutorSchema).min(1),
   306	  concurrency: z.number().int().positive().default(2),
   307	});
   308	
   309	export type FanoutConfig = z.infer<typeof fanoutConfigSchema>;
   310	
   311	/**
   312	 * Parse and validate a raw fan-out configuration.
   313	 *
   314	 * Each entry's executor subset is routed through {@link parseExecutorConfig} so
   315	 * ALL existing kind/model/flag rules (cli-codex needs model, gemini/devin model
   316	 * whitelists, per-kind flag support) apply per lineage with zero duplication.
   317	 * Labels must be unique and base-label (count>1) expansion must not collide.
   318	 *
   319	 * @param raw - Raw input to parse (JSON-parsed object).
   320	 * @returns Validated FanoutConfig.
   321	 * @throws {@link ExecutorConfigError} If validation fails.
   322	 */
   323	export function parseFanoutConfig(raw: unknown): FanoutConfig {
   324	  const parsed = fanoutConfigSchema.safeParse(raw);
   325	  if (!parsed.success) {
   326	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({ issues: parsed.error.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: issue.path, message: issue.message })) });
   327	  }
   328	
   329	  const config = parsed.data;
   330	
   331	  // Reuse the canonical single-executor validator per entry (kind/model/flags).
   332	  config.executors.forEach((entry, index) => {
   333	    const { label: _label, count: _count, iterations: _iterations, ...executorSubset } = entry;
   334	    try {
   335	      parseExecutorConfig(executorSubset);
   336	    } catch (err) {
   337	      if (err instanceof ExecutorConfigError) {
   338	        throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   339	          issues: err.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: ['executors', index, ...issue.path], message: issue.message })),
   340	        });
   341	      }
   342	      throw err;
   343	    }
   344	  });
   345	
   346	  // Labels must be unique among the declared lineages.
   347	  const seenLabels = new Set<string>();
   348	  config.executors.forEach((entry, index) => {
   349	    if (seenLabels.has(entry.label)) {
   350	      throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   351	        issues: [{ path: ['executors', index, 'label'], message: `duplicate lineage label '${entry.label}'` }],
   352	      });
   353	    }
   354	    seenLabels.add(entry.label);
   355	  });
   356	
   357	  // Expanded labels (count>1 → label-1, label-2, …) must not collide either.
   358	  const expandedLabels = new Set<string>();
   359	  for (const lineage of expandLineages(config)) {
   360	    if (expandedLabels.has(lineage.label)) {
   361	      throw new ExecutorConfigError({
   362	        issues: [{ path: ['executors'], message: `expanded lineage label '${lineage.label}' collides; rename base labels` }],
   363	      });
   364	    }
   365	    expandedLabels.add(lineage.label);
   366	  }
   367	
   368	  return config;
   369	}
   370	
   371	/**
   372	 * Expand a fan-out config into concrete per-replica lineages.
   373	 *
   374	 * A `count` of 1 keeps the base label; `count > 1` yields `${label}-1` …
   375	 * `${label}-N`. Each expanded lineage carries `count: 1` so downstream
   376	 * consumers see one runnable unit per element.
   377	 *
   378	 * @param config - Validated FanoutConfig.
   379	 * @returns Flat list of single-replica lineages (one runnable unit each).
   380	 */

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs | sed -n '1,380p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts | sed -n '380,460p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
   380	 */
   381	export function expandLineages(config: FanoutConfig): LineageExecutor[] {
   382	  const lineages: LineageExecutor[] = [];
   383	  for (const entry of config.executors) {
   384	    if (entry.count === 1) {
   385	      lineages.push({ ...entry, count: 1 });
   386	      continue;
   387	    }
   388	    for (let replica = 1; replica <= entry.count; replica += 1) {
   389	      lineages.push({ ...entry, label: `${entry.label}-${replica}`, count: 1 });
   390	    }
   391	  }
   392	  return lineages;
   393	}

     1	#!/usr/bin/env node
     2	
     3	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     4	// ║ Deep-Loop Runtime — Fan-Out CLI Lineage Pool Runner                      ║
     5	// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
     6	// ║ Input:  CLI args (--spec-folder, --loop-type, --fanout-config-json,      ║

     8	// ║ Output: JSON to stdout.                                                  ║
     9	// ║ Exit:   0=all ok, 1=script error, 2=some failed, 3=all failed.          ║

    12	'use strict';

    18	const fs = require('node:fs');
    19	const os = require('node:os');
    20	const path = require('node:path');
    21	const { spawnSync } = require('node:child_process');

    39	
    40	const TSX_LOADER = path.resolve(
    41	  __dirname,
    42	  '..',
    43	  '..',
    44	  'system-spec-kit',
    45	  'scripts',
    46	  'node_modules',
    47	  'tsx',
    48	  'dist',
    49	  'loader.mjs',
    50	);
    51	
    52	if (process.env.DEEP_LOOP_TSX_LOADED !== '1') {
    53	  const child = spawnSync(
    54	    process.execPath,
    55	    ['--import', TSX_LOADER, __filename, ...process.argv.slice(2)],
    56	    {
    57	      cwd: process.cwd(),
    58	      env: { ...process.env, DEEP_LOOP_TSX_LOADED: '1' },
    59	      input: process.stdin.isTTY ? undefined : fs.readFileSync(0),
    60	      encoding: 'utf8',
    61	    },
    62	  );
    63	  if (child.stdout) process.stdout.write(child.stdout);
    64	  if (child.stderr) process.stderr.write(child.stderr);
    65	  process.exit(child.status === null ? 1 : child.status);
    66	}

    68	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    69	// 3. HELPERS
    70	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    71	
    72	function parseArgs(argv = process.argv.slice(2)) {
    73	  const args = {};
    74	  for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i += 1) {
    75	    const token = argv[i];
    76	    if (!token.startsWith('--')) {
    77	      throw inputError(`Unexpected positional argument: ${token}`);
    78	    }
    79	    const key = token.slice(2).replace(/-([a-z])/g, (_, c) => c.toUpperCase());
    80	    const next = argv[i + 1];
    81	    if (next === undefined || next.startsWith('--')) {
    82	      args[key] = true;
    83	    } else {
    84	      args[key] = next;
    85	      i += 1;
    86	    }
    87	  }
    88	  return args;
    89	}
    90	
    91	function inputError(message) {
    92	  const err = new Error(message);
    93	  err.code = 'INPUT_VALIDATION';
    94	  return err;
    95	}
    96	
    97	function ensureString(args, key) {
    98	  if (!args[key] || typeof args[key] !== 'string') {
    99	    throw inputError(`${key} is required`);
   100	  }
   101	  return args[key];
   102	}
   103	
   104	function jsonOut(payload) {
   105	  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(payload)}\n`);
   106	}
   107	
   108	// Per-kind first state-dir env var (used for lockfile isolation across same-kind replicas).
   109	const SPECKIT_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND = {
   110	  'cli-codex': 'SPECKIT_CODEX_STATE_DIR',
   111	  'cli-gemini': 'SPECKIT_GEMINI_STATE_DIR',
   112	  'cli-claude-code': 'SPECKIT_CLAUDE_CODE_STATE_DIR',
   113	  'cli-opencode': 'SPECKIT_OPENCODE_STATE_DIR',
   114	  'cli-devin': 'SPECKIT_DEVIN_STATE_DIR',
   115	};
   116	
   117	/**
   118	 * Build the "run the full loop" prompt text for a CLI lineage subprocess.
   119	 * The subprocess reads this, loads the skill, and runs the loop to convergence
   120	 * with config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir overriding step_resolve_artifact_root.
   121	 */
   122	function buildLoopPrompt(loopType, specFolder, lineageDir, sessionId, lineage) {
   123	  const skillFile =
   124	    loopType === 'review'
   125	      ? '.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md'
   126	      : '.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md';
   127	  const agentName = loopType === 'review' ? 'deep-review' : 'deep-research';
   128	  return [
   129	    `You are a ${agentName} agent running a fan-out lineage.`,
   130	    ``,
   131	    `Read ${skillFile} and execute the ${loopType} loop with these parameters:`,
   132	    `  spec_folder: ${specFolder}`,
   133	    `  config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir: ${lineageDir}`,
   134	    `  session_id: ${sessionId}`,
   135	    `  executor: ${lineage.kind} model=${lineage.model || 'default'}`,
   136	    `  loop_type: ${loopType}`,
   137	    ``,
   138	    `The step_resolve_artifact_root step will bind artifact_dir to ${lineageDir} via the`,
   139	    `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir override — do NOT run the resolveArtifactRoot node`,
   140	    `command; bind artifact_dir directly to the override value.`,
   141	    ``,
   142	    `Run phase_init, phase_main_loop (to convergence), and phase_synthesis.`,
   143	    `Write all outputs to ${lineageDir}. Do not touch any path outside ${lineageDir}.`,
   144	    ``,
   145	    `When complete, output a single line: FANOUT_LINEAGE_COMPLETE:${lineage.label}`,
   146	  ].join('\n');
   147	}
   148	
   149	/**
   150	 * Compute a generous timeout for a full lineage loop run.
   151	 * A single iteration is timeoutSeconds; a full loop is up to iterations * timeoutSeconds.
   152	 * We double it for safety and cap at 4 hours.
   153	 */
   154	function computeLineageTimeoutMs(lineage) {
   155	  const iters = lineage.iterations || 12;
   156	  const perIterSecs = lineage.timeoutSeconds || 900;
   157	  return Math.min(iters * perIterSecs * 2 * 1000, 4 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
   158	}
   159	
   160	/**
   161	 * Build the subprocess command for a CLI lineage.
   162	 * Returns { command, args, input?, env } where input is stdin content when applicable.
   163	 */
   164	function buildLineageCommand(lineage, prompt, resolvedSandbox, resolvedPermission) {
   165	  const kind = lineage.kind;
   166	
   167	  if (kind === 'cli-codex') {
   168	    return {
   169	      command: 'codex',
   170	      args: [
   171	        'exec',
   172	        '--model',
   173	        lineage.model || 'o4-mini',
   174	        '-c',
   175	        `model_reasoning_effort=${lineage.reasoningEffort || 'medium'}`,
   176	        '-c',
   177	        `service_tier=${lineage.serviceTier || 'default'}`,
   178	        '-c',
   179	        'approval_policy=never',
   180	        '--sandbox',
   181	        resolvedSandbox,
   182	        '-',
   183	      ],
   184	      input: prompt,
   185	    };
   186	  }
   187	
   188	  if (kind === 'cli-gemini') {
   189	    return {
   190	      command: 'gemini',
   191	      args: [prompt, '-m', lineage.model || 'gemini-2.5-pro', '-s', resolvedSandbox, '-y', '-o', 'text'],
   192	    };
   193	  }
   194	
   195	  if (kind === 'cli-claude-code') {
   196	    const args = [
   197	      '-p',
   198	      prompt,
   199	      '--model',
   200	      lineage.model || 'claude-opus-4-8',
   201	      '--permission-mode',
   202	      resolvedPermission,
   203	      '--output-format',
   204	      'text',
   205	    ];
   206	    if (lineage.reasoningEffort) {
   207	      args.push('--effort', lineage.reasoningEffort);
   208	    }
   209	    return { command: 'claude', args };
   210	  }
   211	
   212	  if (kind === 'cli-opencode') {
   213	    const args = [
   214	      'run',
   215	      '--model',
   216	      lineage.model || 'anthropic/claude-opus-4-8',
   217	      '--agent',
   218	      'general',
   219	      '--format',
   220	      'json',
   221	      '--dangerously-skip-permissions',
   222	      '--pure',
   223	    ];
   224	    if (lineage.reasoningEffort) {
   225	      args.push('--variant', lineage.reasoningEffort);
   226	    }
   227	    args.push(prompt);
   228	    return { command: 'opencode', args, input: '' };
   229	  }
   230	
   231	  if (kind === 'cli-devin') {
   232	    return {
   233	      command: 'devin',
   234	      args: [
   235	        '--print',
   236	        '--model',
   237	        lineage.model || 'swe-1.6',
   238	        '--permission-mode',
   239	        resolvedPermission,
   240	      ],
   241	      input: prompt,
   242	    };
   243	  }
   244	
   245	  throw inputError(`Unknown CLI executor kind: ${kind}`);
   246	}
   247	
   248	const {
   249	  runSalvageSweep,
   250	  extractTextFromOpencodeJson,
   251	} = require('./fanout-salvage.cjs');
   252	
   253	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   254	// 4. CORE LOGIC
   255	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   256	
   257	async function main() {
   258	  const args = parseArgs();
   259	  const specFolder = validateNamespaceValue(ensureString(args, 'specFolder'), 'specFolder', inputError);
   260	  const loopType = ensureString(args, 'loopType');
   261	  if (loopType !== 'research' && loopType !== 'review') {
   262	    throw inputError('loopType must be "research" or "review"');
   263	  }
   264	  const fanoutConfigJson = ensureString(args, 'fanoutConfigJson');
   265	  const baseArtifactDir = ensureString(args, 'baseArtifactDir');
   266	
   267	  const {
   268	    parseFanoutConfig,
   269	    expandLineages,
   270	    resolveCodexSandboxMode,
   271	    resolveClaudePermissionMode,
   272	    resolveGeminiSandboxMode,
   273	    resolveDevinPermissionMode,
   274	  } = await import('../lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts');
   275	
   276	  installSignalHandlers(() => {});
   277	  maybeThrowTestFault();
   278	
   279	  let rawConfig;
   280	  try {
   281	    rawConfig = JSON.parse(fanoutConfigJson);
   282	  } catch {
   283	    throw inputError('fanoutConfigJson is not valid JSON');
   284	  }
   285	
   286	  const fanoutConfig = parseFanoutConfig(rawConfig);
   287	  const allLineages = expandLineages(fanoutConfig);
   288	
   289	  // Fan-out pool handles CLI lineages only; native lineages are dispatched by the YAML step.
   290	  const cliLineages = allLineages.filter((l) => l.kind !== 'native');
   291	
   292	  if (cliLineages.length === 0) {
   293	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no CLI lineages to spawn', results: [], summary: { total: 0, succeeded: 0, failed: 0, all_failed: false } });
   294	    return;
   295	  }
   296	
   297	  const lineagesDir = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'lineages');
   298	  const ledgerPath = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'orchestration-status.log');
   299	  const summaryPath = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'orchestration-summary.json');
   300	  const runId = `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`;
   301	
   302	  fs.mkdirSync(lineagesDir, { recursive: true });
   303	
   304	  const { results, summary } = await runCappedPool({
   305	    items: cliLineages,
   306	    concurrency: fanoutConfig.concurrency,
   307	    onEvent: (event) => appendStatusLedger(ledgerPath, event),
   308	    worker: async (lineage) => {
   309	      const lineageDir = path.join(lineagesDir, lineage.label);
   310	      const stateDir = path.join(lineageDir, '.executor-state');
   311	      fs.mkdirSync(lineageDir, { recursive: true });
   312	      fs.mkdirSync(stateDir, { recursive: true });
   313	
   314	      const sessionId = `fanout-${lineage.label}-${runId}`;
   315	      const prompt = buildLoopPrompt(loopType, specFolder, lineageDir, sessionId, lineage);
   316	
   317	      const resolvedSandbox = resolveCodexSandboxMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   318	      const resolvedPermission = resolveClaudePermissionMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   319	      const resolvedGeminiSandbox = resolveGeminiSandboxMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   320	      const resolvedDevinPermission = resolveDevinPermissionMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   321	
   322	      const effectiveSandbox = lineage.kind === 'cli-gemini' ? resolvedGeminiSandbox : resolvedSandbox;
   323	      const effectivePermission = lineage.kind === 'cli-devin' ? resolvedDevinPermission : resolvedPermission;
   324	
   325	      const { command, args: cmdArgs, input } = buildLineageCommand(
   326	        lineage,
   327	        prompt,
   328	        effectiveSandbox,
   329	        effectivePermission,
   330	      );
   331	
   332	      // Isolate per-kind state dirs so same-kind replicas do not collide on lockfiles.
   333	      const stateEnvKey = SPECKIT_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND[lineage.kind];
   334	      const extraEnv = {
   335	        SPECKIT_FANOUT_LINEAGE_ID: lineage.label,
   336	        ...(stateEnvKey ? { [stateEnvKey]: stateDir } : {}),
   337	      };
   338	
   339	      const timeoutMs = computeLineageTimeoutMs(lineage);
   340	
   341	      const result = spawnSync(command, cmdArgs, {
   342	        cwd: process.cwd(),
   343	        encoding: 'utf8',
   344	        timeout: timeoutMs,
   345	        env: { ...process.env, ...extraEnv },
   346	        maxBuffer: 20 * 1024 * 1024,
   347	        ...(typeof input === 'string' ? { input } : {}),
   348	      });
   349	
   350	      // Save subprocess stdout for salvage sweep (write failures in weak CLI executors).
   351	      const logsDir = path.join(lineageDir, 'logs');
   352	      fs.mkdirSync(logsDir, { recursive: true });
   353	      const savedStdout = typeof result.stdout === 'string' ? result.stdout : '';
   354	      fs.writeFileSync(path.join(logsDir, 'fanout-lineage.out'), savedStdout, 'utf8');
   355	
   356	      // Recover missing iteration files from captured stdout when possible.
   357	      const salvage = runSalvageSweep(lineageDir, loopType, savedStdout);
   358	
   359	      const exitCode = result.status ?? (result.error ? 1 : 0);
   360	      return { label: lineage.label, exitCode, timedOut: result.signal === 'SIGTERM', salvage };
   361	    },
   362	  });
   363	
   364	  writeOrchestrationSummary(summaryPath, {
   365	    run_id: runId,
   366	    loop_type: loopType,
   367	    spec_folder: specFolder,
   368	    base_artifact_dir: baseArtifactDir,
   369	    total_cli_lineages: cliLineages.length,
   370	    ...summary,
   371	  });
   372	
   373	  const exitCode = summary.all_failed ? 3 : summary.failed > 0 ? 2 : 0;
   374	  jsonOut({ status: exitCode === 0 ? 'ok' : 'partial', run_id: runId, results, summary });
   375	  process.exit(exitCode);
   376	}
   377	
   378	main().catch((err) => {
   379	  const code = classifyExitCode(err);
   380	  jsonOut({

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-pool.cjs | sed -n '1,230p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     2	// ║ Deep-Loop Runtime — Fan-Out Worker Pool                                   ║
     3	// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
     4	// ║ Concurrency-capped fan-out primitive for the opt-in multi-executor        ║
     5	// ║ ("fan-out") layer above the single-executor deep-loop. Generalizes the    ║
     6	// ║ council parallel dispatcher (lib/council/multi-seat-dispatch.cjs) by      ║
     7	// ║ adding a concurrency cap so N executor lineages run with at most K in      ║
     8	// ║ flight, plus a status-ledger writer mirroring the proven packet-122       ║
     9	// ║ orchestration-status.log.                                                  ║
    10	// ║                                                                           ║
    11	// ║ Design: pure pool primitive (worker is INJECTED) + ledger helpers, fully  ║
    12	// ║ unit-tested. The real spawn worker and the CLI entry that wires it        ║
    13	// ║ (arg parse, JSON-out, exit codes) live in fanout-run.cjs.                ║
    14	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
    15	
    16	'use strict';
    17	
    18	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    19	// 1. IMPORTS
    20	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    21	
    22	const fs = require('node:fs');
    23	const path = require('node:path');
    24	
    25	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    26	// 2. HELPERS
    27	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    28	
    29	function isRecord(value) {
    30	  return value !== null && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value);
    31	}
    32	
    33	function normalizeTimestamp(value) {
    34	  if (value instanceof Date) {
    35	    return value.toISOString();
    36	  }
    37	  if (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() !== '') {
    38	    return value;
    39	  }
    40	  return new Date().toISOString();
    41	}
    42	
    43	function normalizeConcurrency(value) {
    44	  const n = Number(value);
    45	  if (!Number.isFinite(n) || n < 1) {
    46	    return 1;
    47	  }
    48	  return Math.floor(n);
    49	}
    50	
    51	function labelFor(item, index) {
    52	  if (isRecord(item) && typeof item.label === 'string' && item.label.trim() !== '') {
    53	    return item.label;
    54	  }
    55	  return `item-${index}`;
    56	}
    57	
    58	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    59	// 3. CORE LOGIC
    60	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    61	
    62	/**
    63	 * Run a single pool item through the worker and capture its result or error.
    64	 *
    65	 * Never throws: a worker rejection is captured as a `rejected` result so one
    66	 * failing lineage cannot sink the whole pool (mirrors council `settleSeat`).
    67	 *
    68	 * @param {Object} params - Settlement parameters.
    69	 * @param {*} params.item - The work item (e.g. a lineage descriptor).
    70	 * @param {number} params.index - Zero-based item index (preserves order).
    71	 * @param {Function} params.worker - Async worker: (item, { index }) => output.
    72	 * @param {Function} params.now - Callable returning a Date or ISO timestamp.
    73	 * @param {Function} [params.onEvent] - Optional callback for ledger events.
    74	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} Result with label, status, timing, output|error.
    75	 */
    76	async function settleItem({ item, index, worker, now, onEvent }) {
    77	  const label = labelFor(item, index);
    78	  const startedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
    79	  const startedAtMs = Date.now();
    80	
    81	  if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
    82	    onEvent({ event: 'started', label, index, at: startedAtIso });
    83	  }
    84	
    85	  try {
    86	    const output = await worker(item, { index });
    87	    const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
    88	    const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs);
    89	    if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
    90	      onEvent({ event: 'completed', label, index, at: completedAtIso, duration_ms: durationMs });
    91	    }
    92	    return {
    93	      label,
    94	      status: 'fulfilled',
    95	      started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
    96	      completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
    97	      duration_ms: durationMs,
    98	      output,
    99	    };
   100	  } catch (error) {
   101	    const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
   102	    const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs);
   103	    const normalizedError = {
   104	      name: error && error.name ? String(error.name) : 'Error',
   105	      message: error && error.message ? String(error.message) : String(error),
   106	    };
   107	    if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
   108	      onEvent({ event: 'failed', label, index, at: completedAtIso, duration_ms: durationMs, error: normalizedError });
   109	    }
   110	    return {
   111	      label,
   112	      status: 'rejected',
   113	      started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
   114	      completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
   115	      duration_ms: durationMs,
   116	      error: normalizedError,
   117	    };
   118	  }
   119	}
   120	
   121	/**
   122	 * Run items through a worker with at most `concurrency` in flight.
   123	 *
   124	 * This is the fan-out generalization of council `dispatchCouncilSeats`: same
   125	 * never-throws per-item settlement and ordered results, plus a concurrency cap
   126	 * so a large lineage set runs K-at-a-time (the proven `xargs -P K` shape).
   127	 *
   128	 * @param {Object} options - Pool configuration.
   129	 * @param {Array} options.items - Work items (each ideally has a `label`).
   130	 * @param {number} options.concurrency - Max items in flight (clamped to >= 1).
   131	 * @param {Function} options.worker - Async worker: (item, { index }) => output.
   132	 * @param {Function} [options.now] - Callable returning a Date/ISO timestamp.
   133	 * @param {Function} [options.onEvent] - Optional ledger-event callback.
   134	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} { results: [...ordered], summary: { total,
   135	 *   succeeded, failed, all_failed } }.
   136	 * @throws {TypeError} If items is not an array or worker is not a function.
   137	 */
   138	function runCappedPool(options) {
   139	  if (!isRecord(options)) {
   140	    throw new TypeError('runCappedPool options must be an object');
   141	  }
   142	  const { items, worker } = options;
   143	  if (!Array.isArray(items)) {
   144	    throw new TypeError('items must be an array');
   145	  }
   146	  if (typeof worker !== 'function') {
   147	    throw new TypeError('worker must be a function');
   148	  }
   149	
   150	  const concurrency = normalizeConcurrency(options.concurrency);
   151	  const now = typeof options.now === 'function' ? options.now : () => new Date();
   152	  const onEvent = typeof options.onEvent === 'function' ? options.onEvent : undefined;
   153	  const results = new Array(items.length);
   154	
   155	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
   156	    if (items.length === 0) {
   157	      resolve(buildPoolSummary(results));
   158	      return;
   159	    }
   160	
   161	    let nextIndex = 0;
   162	    let active = 0;
   163	    let resolved = false;
   164	
   165	    const pump = () => {
   166	      if (resolved) {
   167	        return;
   168	      }
   169	      if (nextIndex >= items.length && active === 0) {
   170	        resolved = true;
   171	        resolve(buildPoolSummary(results));
   172	        return;
   173	      }
   174	      while (active < concurrency && nextIndex < items.length) {
   175	        const index = nextIndex;
   176	        nextIndex += 1;
   177	        active += 1;
   178	        settleItem({ item: items[index], index, worker, now, onEvent })
   179	          .then((result) => {
   180	            results[index] = result;
   181	          })
   182	          .catch((error) => {
   183	            // settleItem never throws, but stay defensive so the pump never wedges.
   184	            results[index] = {
   185	              label: labelFor(items[index], index),
   186	              status: 'rejected',
   187	              error: { name: 'PoolError', message: String(error) },
   188	            };
   189	          })
   190	          .finally(() => {
   191	            active -= 1;
   192	            pump();
   193	          });
   194	      }
   195	    };
   196	
   197	    pump();
   198	  });
   199	}
   200	
   201	/**
   202	 * Build the ordered-results + summary envelope for a completed pool run.
   203	 *
   204	 * @param {Array<Object>} results - Per-item settlement results (ordered).
   205	 * @returns {Object} { results, summary: { total, succeeded, failed, all_failed } }.
   206	 */
   207	function buildPoolSummary(results) {
   208	  const total = results.length;
   209	  const succeeded = results.filter((result) => result && result.status === 'fulfilled').length;
   210	  const failed = total - succeeded;
   211	  return {
   212	    results,
   213	    summary: {
   214	      total,
   215	      succeeded,
   216	      failed,
   217	      all_failed: total > 0 && failed === total,
   218	    },
   219	  };
   220	}
   221	
   222	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   223	// 4. STATUS LEDGER
   224	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   225	
   226	/**
   227	 * Append one JSONL event to the orchestration status ledger.
   228	 *
   229	 * Generalizes the packet-122 `orchestration-status.log`: one line per lineage
   230	 * lifecycle event (started / completed / failed / salvaged / converged).

   100	  iteration_pattern: "{artifact_dir}/iterations/iteration-{NNN}.md"

   103	  # like `"iteration_delta"` are silently dropped by the reducer.

   108	  archive_root: "{artifact_archive_root}"
   109	
   110	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   111	# WORKFLOW
   112	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   113	workflow:
   114	
   115	  step_resolve_artifact_root:
   116	    description: "Resolve artifact directory — use fan-out lineage override when provided, else resolve from spec_folder"
   117	    note: "Root specs keep review artifacts directly in {spec_folder}/review/. Child phases and sub-phases keep local packet directories in {spec_folder}/review/{packet}/, and archives stay local at {spec_folder}/review_archive/{packet}/."
   118	    branch_on: "config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir"
   119	    if_present:
   120	      bind:
   121	        artifact_dir: "{config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir}"
   122	        artifact_root_dir: "{config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir}"
   123	        artifact_subfolder: null
   124	        artifact_archive_root: "{config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir}/_archive"
   125	    if_absent:
   126	      command: "node -e \"const { resolveArtifactRoot } = require('.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/shared/review-research-paths.cjs'); console.log(JSON.stringify(resolveArtifactRoot('{spec_folder}', 'review')));\""
   127	      outputs:
   128	        - artifact_root_dir: "Resolved local review owner directory"
   129	        - artifact_subfolder: "Local packet subfolder ({ownerSlug}-pt-{NN}), else null"
   130	        - artifact_dir: "Resolved review packet directory for this target"
   131	        - artifact_archive_root: "Resolved local review archive directory for this target"
   132	
   133	  step_fanout_spawn:
   134	    description: "Spawn fan-out lineages; skip and proceed to phase_init normally when single-executor"
   135	    skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
   136	    instructions: |
   137	      Fan-out mode is active. Spawn all lineages. Each lineage runs the full loop in its own
   138	      isolated sub-packet under {artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/.
   139	      After all lineages complete, jump directly to phase_synthesis — skip phase_init and
   140	      phase_main_loop entirely (those are the single-executor path). The merge step in
   141	      phase_synthesis will consolidate results across all lineages.
   142	    sub_steps:
   143	      step_fanout_spawn_cli:
   144	        description: "Spawn all CLI lineages in capped worker pool"
   145	        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind != 'native'"
   146	        command: |
   147	          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs \
   148	            --spec-folder {spec_folder} \
   149	            --loop-type review \
   150	            --fanout-config-json '{config.fanout_json}' \
   151	            --base-artifact-dir {artifact_dir}
   152	        working_directory: "{repo_root}"
   153	        wait_for_completion: true
   154	      step_fanout_spawn_native:
   155	        description: "Dispatch each native lineage as a sequential deep-review sub-agent"
   156	        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind == 'native'"
   157	        for_each: "lineage in expanded native entries of config.fanout"
   158	        dispatch:
   159	          agent: deep-review
   160	          model: opus
   161	          context: |
   162	            Execute the deep-review loop with these parameters:
   163	              spec_folder: {spec_folder}
   164	              config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir: {artifact_dir}/lineages/{lineage.label}
   165	              session_id: fanout-{lineage.label}-{fanout_run_id}
   166	              executor: native
   167	            Run to convergence. Write all outputs to the override directory.
   168	        wait_for_completion: true
   169	
   170	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   171	  # PHASE 1: INITIALIZATION
   172	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   173	  phase_init:
   174	    purpose: Create all state files for the review session and resolve review scope
   175	    steps:
   690	          D) Cancel entirely
   691	        wait_for: "User response"
   692	        on_A: { proceed_to: step_dispatch_iteration }
   693	        on_B: { action: "Update next_dimension to user's choice", proceed_to: step_dispatch_iteration }
   694	        on_C: { proceed_to: gate_pre_synthesis }
   695	        on_D: { action: "Cancel workflow", status: "CANCELLED" }
   696	
   697	      step_dispatch_iteration:
   698	        action: "Dispatch @deep-review agent for one review iteration"
   699	        resolve_executor:
   700	          source: "config.executor"
   701	          loader: ".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts#parseExecutorConfig"
   702	        pre_dispatch:
   703	          generate_summary: |

  1126	        skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
  1127	        command: |
  1128	          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs \

   170	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   171	  # PHASE 1: INITIALIZATION
   172	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   173	  phase_init:
   174	    purpose: Create all state files for the review session and resolve review scope
   175	    steps:
   176	
   177	      step_preflight_contract:
   178	        action: "Validate required setup bindings and executable workflow contract before any file writes"
   179	        verify:
   180	          - required_values_present: [review_target, review_target_type, review_dimensions, spec_folder, max_iterations, convergence_threshold]
   680	      step_marker_scan:
   681	        action: "H-2 loop-prevention guard: scan previous iteration's rendered prompt for nested review marker"
   682	        algorithm: |
   683	          if current_iteration > 1:
   684	            PREV_PROMPT_PATH = "{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{previous_iteration}.md"
   685	            if file_exists(PREV_PROMPT_PATH):
   686	              PREV_PROMPT_FIRST_LINE = head -1 of PREV_PROMPT_PATH
   687	              if PREV_PROMPT_FIRST_LINE matches ^(DEEP-REVIEW|DEEP-RESEARCH|CODE-REVIEW)$:
   688	                log "ERROR: nested review-of-review loop detected (marker found in previous iteration prompt). Aborting."
   689	                halt with exit code 2
   690	        note: |
   691	          False-positive prevention: only matches when the first line is exactly one of the
   692	          three canonical markers. Markers appearing in comments or body text are ignored.
   693	          True-positive triggers only when a previous iteration's rendered prompt literally
   694	          starts with a deep-loop marker, indicating a nested review-of-review dispatch.
   695	
   696	      step_dispatch_iteration:
   697	        action: "Dispatch @deep-review agent for one review iteration"
   698	        resolve_executor:
   699	          source: "config.executor"
   700	          loader: ".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts#parseExecutorConfig"
   701	        pre_dispatch:
   702	          generate_summary: |
   703	            STATE SUMMARY (auto-generated):
   704	            Iteration: {current_iteration} of {max_iterations}
   705	            Dimension: {next_dimension}
   706	            Prior Findings: P0={p0_count} P1={p1_count} P2={p2_count}
   707	            Dimension Coverage: {dimensions_covered_list} ({dimension_coverage})
   708	            Traceability: core={core_protocol_status} overlay={overlay_protocol_status}
   709	            {resource_map_prompt_line}
   710	            Coverage Age: {coverage_age}
   711	            Last 2 ratios: {ratio_prev} -> {ratio_latest}
   712	            Stuck count: {stuck_count}
   713	            Provisional Verdict: {PASS|CONDITIONAL|FAIL|PENDING} hasAdvisories={has_advisories}
   714	          render_prompt_pack:
   715	            template_path: ".opencode/skills/deep-review/assets/prompt_pack_iteration.md.tmpl"
   716	            renderer: ".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/prompt-pack.ts#renderPromptPack"
   717	            output_path: "{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{current_iteration}.md"
   718	            variables:
   719	              state_summary: "{state_summary}"
   720	              current_iteration: "{current_iteration}"

   165	      step_fanout_spawn_native:
   166	        description: "Dispatch each native lineage as a sequential deep-research sub-agent"
   167	        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind == 'native'"
   168	        for_each: "lineage in expanded native entries of config.fanout"
   169	        dispatch:
   170	          agent: deep-research
   171	          model: opus
   172	          context: |
   173	            Execute the deep-research loop with these parameters:
   174	              spec_folder: {spec_folder}
   175	              config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir: {artifact_dir}/lineages/{lineage.label}
   176	              session_id: fanout-{lineage.label}-{fanout_run_id}
   177	              executor: native
   178	            Run to convergence. Write all outputs to the override directory.
   179	        wait_for_completion: true
   180	
   575	          False-positive prevention: only matches when the first line is exactly one of the
   576	          three canonical markers. Markers appearing in comments or body text are ignored.
   577	          True-positive triggers only when a previous iteration's rendered prompt literally
   578	          starts with a deep-loop marker, indicating a nested research-of-review dispatch.
   579	
   580	      step_dispatch_iteration:
   581	        action: "Dispatch one iteration via the selected executor"
   582	        resolve_executor:
   583	          source: "config.executor"
   584	          loader: ".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts#parseExecutorConfig"
   585	        pre_dispatch:
   586	          generate_summary: |
   587	            STATE SUMMARY (auto-generated):
   588	            Segment: {current_segment} | Iteration: {current_iteration} of {max_iterations}
   589	            Questions: {answered_count}/{total_questions} answered | Last focus: {last_focus}
   590	            Last 2 ratios: {ratio_prev} -> {ratio_latest} | Stuck count: {stuck_count}
   956	  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   957	  phase_synthesis:
   958	    purpose: Compile all iteration findings into final research/research.md
   959	    steps:
   960	
   961	      step_fanout_merge:
   962	        description: "Merge N lineage registries into one consolidated registry; skip when single-executor"
   963	        skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
   964	        command: |
   965	          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs \

   967	            --artifact-dir {artifact_dir}

   969	        wait_for_completion: true
   970	
   971	      step_hydrate_summary_metrics:
   972	        action: "Hydrate summary metrics before synthesis"

codex
The runtime has a real `parseFanoutConfig` path after YAML hands it JSON, but the Markdown command entrypoints still own parsing `$ARGUMENTS` into either `config.executor` or `config.fanout`. I’m checking tests now to separate missing behavior from merely missing documentation.

     1	import { existsSync, mkdtempSync, readFileSync, rmSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
     2	import { tmpdir } from 'node:os';

     5	import { afterEach, describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
     6	
     7	import { runtimeRoot, spawnCjs } from '../helpers/spawn-cjs';
     8	
     9	const tempDirs: string[] = [];
    10	
    11	function makeTempDir(prefix: string): string {
    12	  const dir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), prefix));
    13	  tempDirs.push(dir);
    14	  return dir;
    15	}
    16	
    17	/**
    18	 * Write a stub binary at `binDir/{name}` that exits 0.
    19	 * Used to simulate headless CLI executors without real API keys.
    20	 */
    21	function writeStubBinary(binDir: string, name: string): string {
    22	  const stubPath = join(binDir, name);
    23	  writeFileSync(stubPath, '#!/bin/sh\necho "stub-done"\nexit 0\n', { mode: 0o755 });
    24	  return stubPath;
    25	}
    26	
    27	afterEach(() => {
    28	  while (tempDirs.length > 0) {
    29	    const dir = tempDirs.pop();
    30	    if (dir) rmSync(dir, { recursive: true, force: true });
    31	  }
    32	});

 succeeded in 0ms:
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:3:description: "Design (spec only) a first-class contract-compliant per-iteration model schedule for deep-loop fan-out: an operator requests a fixed-count heterogeneous schedule (e.g. nine iterations on minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed then one on claude-opus-4-8) via /deep:start-review-loop, /deep:start-research-loop, and shared deep-loop-runtime, so future runs never hand-roll a worker pool like packet 122 review did. Models the schedule as ordered single-executor lineages (bands) over existing fan-out via a thin iterationPlan flag desugaring one-to-one into ordered lineages plus minimal sequencing. Produces schema, routing, audit, merge-precedence, command-surface design; implementation is this phase's future work."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:40:- Contract-compliance and backward-compat design: satisfy every deep-review forbidden pattern (no ad-hoc shell fan-out; no direct CLI loop; no `/tmp` prompt dispatch; no Task-tool agent dispatch; no skipping the JSONL state machine; state under the local review/research packet); preserve read-only, externalized-state, and per-iteration-JSONL contracts; keep single-executor and homogeneous fan-out byte-identical with opt-in absent-by-default fields.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:49:- New executor kinds/providers/models beyond those registered (minimax, codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin, native) and changes to the single-executor default path or existing homogeneous fan-out merge semantics.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/spec.md:63:- **Merge-precedence regression:** generalizing the any-P0-to-FAIL rule into ordered band-priority risks weakening strongest-restriction, since a lower-priority band P0 must still escalate. Mitigation: specify precedence as conflict-resolution only, with severity rollup preserved as an invariant above band priority.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/001-schema-config-plumbing/spec.md:3:description: "Add multi-executor fan-out config schema (lineageExecutorSchema, fanoutConfigSchema, parseFanoutConfig, expandLineages) to deep-loop-runtime executor-config.ts + optional lineageId in executor-audit; no behavior change; single-executor parity preserved."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/001-schema-config-plumbing/spec.md:35:- New unit tests cover fan-out parse / both-present conflict / count-expansion / per-entry kind validation reuse.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/001-schema-config-plumbing/spec.md:36:- **Parity gate:** existing single-executor `parseExecutorConfig` tests byte-identical; full vitest green.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/006-command-surface-docs-parity/spec.md:3:description: "Command flag parsing (--executor repeatable, --executors json, --concurrency) + default policy (single-executor default) in both deep command entrypoints; SKILL/convergence/runtime doc updates permitting command-driven fan-out (wave stays deferred); final byte-identical single-executor parity gate."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/006-command-surface-docs-parity/spec.md:27:Expose fan-out via the command entrypoints (opt-in), document it for both consumers, and prove single-executor parity.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/006-command-surface-docs-parity/spec.md:30:- `commands/deep/start-research-loop.md` + `start-review-loop.md` §0: repeatable `--executor` (groups trailing `--model/--reasoning-effort/--service-tier/--executor-timeout/--iters/--label/--count`), `--executors <json>` escape hatch, `--concurrency N`; Default Resolution Table + PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS additions; fan-out EXAMPLES.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/006-command-surface-docs-parity/spec.md:31:- Default policy: 0–1 executor & no `--executors` ⇒ `config.executor` (single, recommended default, unchanged); 2+ / `--executors` / `count>1` ⇒ `config.fanout`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/006-command-surface-docs-parity/spec.md:35:- **Final parity gate (non-negotiable):** single-executor run byte-identical to pre-change `main` (config, state.jsonl modulo timestamps, iteration md, research.md/review-report.md).

.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:116:**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Review fan-out uses strongest-restriction: any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-review` sub-agents; CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses in the capped pool.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:148:   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:149:   |-- --executors=<json> -> config.fanout.executors = parse(json) escape hatch
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:150:   |-- --concurrency=N -> config.fanout.concurrency (default 2)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-review-loop.md:154:   |   - 2+ --executor flags, --executors, or any count>1: write config.fanout
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:107:**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-research` agent dispatches with isolated dirs. CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses concurrently in the capped pool.
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:135:   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:136:   |-- --executors=<json> -> config.fanout.executors = parse(json) escape hatch for complex configs
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:137:   |-- --concurrency=N -> config.fanout.concurrency (default 2)
.opencode/commands/deep/start-research-loop.md:141:   |   - 2+ --executor flags, --executors, or any count>1: write config.fanout
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/006-command-surface-docs-parity/spec.md:31:- Default policy: 0–1 executor & no `--executors` ⇒ `config.executor` (single, recommended default, unchanged); 2+ / `--executors` / `count>1` ⇒ `config.fanout`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:60:- **Backward-compat rule:** config carries EITHER `config.executor` (single) OR `config.fanout` (multi), never both. The both-present guard is deferred to Phase 6 (command surface) — no call site writes `config.fanout` yet.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:85:  - Override logic: if `--artifact-dir-override` / `config.fanout` sub-packet dir is present → bind `artifact_dir` to it; else run the existing inline resolver unchanged.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:96:- New `step_fanout_merge` at the TOP of each `phase_synthesis`, gated on `config.fanout` present (absent ⇒ synthesis unchanged):
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/handover.md:103:- `commands/deep/start-research-loop.md` + `start-review-loop.md` §0: repeatable `--executor` (groups trailing `--model/--reasoning-effort/--service-tier/--executor-timeout/--iters/--label/--count`), `--executors <json>` escape hatch, `--concurrency N`; extend the Default Resolution Table + PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS; add fan-out EXAMPLES. **Default policy: 0–1 executor & no `--executors` ⇒ write `config.executor` (single, unchanged, documented as the recommended default); 2+ / `--executors` / any `count>1` ⇒ write `config.fanout`.** Add the both-present fail-fast here.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/003-per-lineage-spawn-isolation/decision-record.md:72:The key enabling mechanism for all types: `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/003-per-lineage-spawn-isolation/decision-record.md:107:- `step_resolve_artifact_root` in all 4 YAMLs gains a `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/005-consumer-merges-synthesis/spec.md:31:- New `step_fanout_merge` at the TOP of each `phase_synthesis` (4 YAMLs), gated on `config.fanout`:
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/001-schema-config-plumbing/spec.md:31:- Backward-compat: config carries EITHER `config.executor` OR `config.fanout`, never both; both-present fails fast.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/001-schema-config-plumbing/implementation-summary.md:50:Config carries EITHER `config.executor` (single, default, unchanged) OR `config.fanout` (multi). No call site writes both yet; the both-present guard lands with the command surface in Phase 006.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:40:- **`step_resolve_artifact_root` updated in all 4 YAMLs** — Added `branch_on: "config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir"`. When the override is present (a lineage subprocess received it), `artifact_dir` is bound directly — no `resolveArtifactRoot` node call. When absent (single-executor mode), the `if_absent` branch runs the byte-identical original command. Affects `deep_start-{research,review}-loop_{auto,confirm}.yaml`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:42:- **`step_fanout_spawn` added to all 4 YAMLs** — New top-level workflow step with `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"`. When config.fanout is present: spawns CLI lineages via `fanout-run.cjs` (`step_fanout_spawn_cli`); dispatches native lineages as sequential `agent: deep-research`/`deep-review` runs (`step_fanout_spawn_native`); after all lineages converge, jumps to `phase_synthesis` skipping `phase_init` and `phase_main_loop`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:58:- **`step_fanout_merge` added to top of `phase_synthesis` in all 4 YAMLs** — `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"`. Calls `fanout-merge.cjs` with the base `artifact_dir`. Review variant adds `bind_from_output: {p0_count: "active_p0", p1_count: "active_p1", p2_count: "active_p2"}` so `step_derive_verdict` uses merged counts rather than an empty single-executor state log. Research variant feeds the merged `deep-research-findings-registry.json` into the existing `step_compile_research` unchanged.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:64:- **`commands/deep/start-research-loop.md`** — argument-hint updated with fan-out flags; Default Resolution Table gains `fanout_executors` and `fanout_concurrency` rows; PARSE step 3 gains `--executor` (repeatable, with `--model/--reasoning-effort/--service-tier/--executor-timeout/--iters/--label/--count` per group), `--executors <json>` escape hatch, `--concurrency N`, and the default policy (0–1 executor → `config.executor`; 2+ / `--executors` / `count>1` → `config.fanout`); EXAMPLES updated with 3 fan-out scenarios (2-CLI parallel, native+CLI mixed, JSON escape hatch) and a native fan-out note.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:68:- **`deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md`** — Script table extended with 4 new rows (`fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, `fanout-merge.cjs`). Fan-out isolation invariant documented: per-lineage `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override, per-lineage `session_id` for coverage-graph DB writes (no schema change), per-kind `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` for lockfile isolation.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:101:All fan-out steps are gated on `config.fanout` being present:
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:102:- `step_resolve_artifact_root`: `if_absent` branch runs the byte-identical original `resolveArtifactRoot` node command when `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` is not set.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:103:- `step_fanout_spawn`: `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"` — completely skipped in single mode.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:104:- `step_fanout_merge`: `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"` — completely skipped in single mode.
